content: Agent working memory capacity limits do not arise from fixed storage constraints, but from insufficient spreading activation propagation across weakly connected associative memory traces, and we can mitigate this bottleneck by augmenting agent working memory with a lightweight, on-the-fly spreading activation pass similar to the one used for associative recall in RAG. Spreading activation can dynamically prioritize and activate underconnected relevant memory entries that would otherwise be cut off by arbitrary working memory slot limits, unlocking more consistent multi-step reasoning for long-horizon agent tasks.
source_topics: **Spreading Activation for large language model associative recall
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: **Spreading Activation for large language model associative recall
